translation_findings.jp: 研究段階と開発段階の間にある難関
translation_findings.zh: 研究阶段与开发阶段之间的难关
translation_findings.en: Barrier between Research Stage and Development Stage
translation_findings.issue: EN uses title-case 'Research Stage'/'Development Stage' for what JP renders as common-noun phases. Consistent across 魔の川/死の谷/ダーウィンの海 — stylistic choice that emphasizes them as named phases. Not a defect, just heavier capitalization than typical. Article-less 'Barrier between...' is concise but slightly telegraphic. ZH is natural ('研究阶段与开发阶段之间的难关').
translation_findings.suggested_fix: Optional polish: 'A barrier that lies between the research and development stages' for more natural EN. Current form is acceptable.

translation_findings.jp: キャズム / 商品が市場でシェアを拡大する際に障壁となる溝のこと
translation_findings.zh: 鸿沟 / 商品在市场扩大份额时形成障碍的鸿沟
translation_findings.en: Chasm / The gap that becomes a barrier when a product expands its market share
translation_findings.issue: ZH definition '商品在市场扩大份额时形成障碍的鸿沟' is mildly tautological (surface=鸿沟, definition ends in 鸿沟). EN avoids the tautology by using 'gap'/'barrier'. Faithful to jp's '障壁となる溝のこと' (a 'gap that becomes a barrier') — jp itself uses 溝 which is a near-synonym of キャズム so the tautology is inherited from jp, not introduced. WARN polish only.
translation_findings.suggested_fix: Consider zh: '商品在市场扩大份额时形成障碍的沟壑' or '...扩大份额时遇到的障碍' to avoid term-defines-itself appearance.

translation_findings.issue: ZH renders 'イノベーター理論' as '创新扩散理论' (Diffusion of Innovations Theory — Rogers's actual book title). EN renders as literal 'Innovator Theory'. ZH chose the established Chinese academic term (correct Rogers reference), EN stayed literal. Both are defensible; zh is arguably more correct domain-wise, en is more literal-to-jp. Mild zh/en asymmetry but not contradictory.
translation_findings.suggested_fix: Consider en: 'Diffusion of Innovations Theory (Innovator Theory)' for domain-accuracy parity with zh.

narrative: Section title + 6 term entities (魔の川, 死の谷, ダーウィンの海, キャズム, イノベーター理論, ロードマップ) + 1 figure caption. All translations semantically faithful: the 5-segment Innovator Theory classification (Innovators→Early Adopters→Early Majority→Late Majority→Laggards) is exactly preserved in zh (创新者→早期采用者→早期大众→晚期大众→落后者) and en. ロードマップ definition (axes, periodicity, market sensitivity) renders correctly. WARN-level polish items only: capitalization style in EN barrier definitions, zh tautology inherited from jp, and a zh-vs-en term-selection asymmetry on イノベーター理論. No inversion, no hallucination, no marker issues.
